documentation.readme: This penetration testing tool allows an auditor to intercept SSH connections. A patch applied to the OpenSSH v7.5p1 source code causes it to act as a proxy between the victim and their intended SSH server; all plaintext passwords and sessions are logged to disk.
usage_sections: v2.3: ???: Added support for Linux Mint 20 & Ubuntu 20.
v2.2: September 16, 2019: Fixed installation on Kali & Linux Mint 19. Fixed a double-password prompt that occured under certain conditions. Improved error logging.
v2.0: September 12, 2017: Added full SFTP support(!) and AppArmor confinement.
v1.1: July 6, 2017: Removed root privilege dependencies, added automatic installer, added Kali Linux support, added JoesAwesomeSSHMITMVictimFinder.py script to find potential targets on a LAN.

arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

name: ssh-mitm
summary: This penetration testing tool allows an auditor to intercept SSH connections. A patch applied to the OpenSSH v7.5p1 source code causes it to act as a proxy between the victim and their intended SSH server; all plaintext passwords and sessions are logged to disk.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need frameworks, use ssh-mitm because This penetration testing tool allows an auditor to intercept SSH connections. A patch applied to the OpenSSH v7.5p1 source code causes it to act as a proxy betw.
When chaining frameworks tools, pair ssh-mitm with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ssh-mitm --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
